response.justification: The candidate is a strong polyglot engineer with solid craftsmanship, testing culture, and low-latency systems experience, but the profile fundamentally misses the mandatory requirements: professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 rather than 5-10 years on Windows, SQL experience is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and there is no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain background (RIDE Capital was retail investment tooling in Node/TypeScript, not investment banking). The core stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not transfer directly to this consulting engagement.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, DevOps tooling, and community teaching
Broad polyglot background including Java, C#, and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ work (2014-2015), far short of the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle background; database experience is MongoDB and MySQL

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
